paper_metadata.title: Meta-analyses of the determinants and outcomes of belief in climate change
paper_metadata.authors: Matthew J. Hornsey, Emily A. Harris, Paul G. Bain, Kelly S. Fielding
exact_tax_terms_used: support for carbon tax/cap and trade
paper_metadata.carbon_tax_relevance_summary: Meta-analysis includes a pooled outcome on support for carbon tax/cap-and-trade, making it relevant but not carbon-tax-only.

determinants.definition: Belief that climate change is real and human-caused.
determinants.mechanism: Accepting climate change raises perceived need for mitigation, so carbon pricing seems more justified and acceptable.
determinants.exact_tax_policy_context: Support for carbon tax/cap and trade.
determinants.outcome_variable: Support for carbon tax/cap and trade
determinants.evidence_quote: support for mitigation policies that place a price on carbon (carbon tax or cap and trade)
determinants.notes: Outcome pooled with cap-and-trade; no tax-only estimate.

exclusions_or_uncertainties.reason: The paper reports support for carbon tax/cap and trade combined, not a separate carbon-tax coefficient.
